name: Lazy

dos: Use Migra 58px weight 500 with 0.048em tracking for all section-level display headlines — this is the brand's voice
Set body text at Inter 16px weight 400 with line-height 1.5 for comfortable reading rhythm
Apply the dual-layer shadow stack (rgba(4,4,7,0.25) 0 2px 4px + rgba(4,4,7,0.4) 0 8px 24px) to all floating UI elements like command bars and modals
Maintain 8px radius for cards and 4px radius for buttons — this 2:1 ratio is consistent across the system
Use #8a8c93 as the default border color at 1px width — it is the most-used token in the system (1357 occurrences) and defines visual structure
Center all hero and section content; Lazy is a symmetrical, meditative layout — never left-align primary headlines
Pair every serif headline with a sans-serif subhead in #8a8c93 — the contrast between Migra and Inter creates Lazy's editorial-meets-UI identity
donts: Never introduce chromatic colors — the entire system is achromatic by design; adding a blue or green would break the editorial register
Never use solid-fill CTA buttons; all action buttons are ghost/outlined with #62646a borders to maintain the quiet aesthetic
Never set body text tighter than -0.005em letter-spacing or display text looser than 0.138em — the tracking curve is the system
Never use Migra below 21px — at smaller sizes the serif loses its editorial character and becomes noise
Never add drop shadows stronger than the documented two values — Lazy floats softly, never projects hard edges
Never left-align serif display headlines — they must be centered to maintain the typeset manuscript metaphor
Never use white (#ffffff) as a background; white is reserved for primary text and the note-preview card contrast moment

colors.name: Carbon Canvas
colors.role: Page background, hero canvas, section grounds

colors.name: Obsidian
colors.role: Deepest borders, shadow cores, overlaid text on light surfaces

colors.name: Inkwell
colors.role: Secondary surface tint, input fills, subtle elevation step above canvas

colors.name: Graphite Card
colors.role: Elevated card surfaces, modal panels, floating UI containers

colors.name: Pewter
colors.role: Primary hairline borders, structural dividers, most-used neutral

colors.name: Slate
colors.role: Secondary borders, link underlines, subdued UI strokes

colors.name: Ash
colors.role: Badge borders, tertiary separators, muted structural lines

colors.name: Bone
colors.role: Light card borders, high-contrast dividers, icon strokes on dark surfaces

colors.name: Paper
colors.role: Primary heading text, high-contrast body text, emphasis borders

designSystem.layout: Lazy follows a full-bleed dark canvas with centered content blocks. The page reads as vertical scroll through typographic acts: each section is a centered composition with a serif label, serif headline, sans subhead, and one focal UI element. Max-width is 1200px for the content column, but the canvas extends edge-to-edge. The hero occupies a full viewport with the command bar centered and a radial light effect drawing the eye. Subsequent sections are 80px vertical gaps with the same centered axis. There is no left/right alternation, no sidebar, no grid of cards — each section is a single editorial spread. Navigation is a minimal top bar with no background fill.
designSystem.imagery: Lazy uses almost no traditional imagery — the visual system is type-driven and UI-driven. The only

similar.why: Same centered, single-focal-element-per-section hero pattern with a command bar as the hero UI
Dark-mode productivity tool with extreme typographic restraint and Inter as the UI workhorse font
Full-bleed dark canvas with centered editorial compositions and generous vertical rhythm between sections
Serif-and-sans typography pairing on a dark canvas that reads as editorial rather than SaaS
Monochrome, type-driven visual system where structure and whitespace carry all hierarchy

surfaces.name: Carbon Canvas
surfaces.purpose: Base page background for all sections

surfaces.name: Inkwell
surfaces.purpose: Recessed inputs, subtle inset surfaces, secondary canvas tint

surfaces.name: Obsidian
surfaces.purpose: Deepest border, shadow anchor, maximum contrast for text

surfaces.name: Graphite Card
surfaces.purpose: Elevated card surfaces, floating command bars, modal panels

components.role: Persistent header bar with brand, nav links, auth actions
components.description: Full-width transparent bar over canvas. Logo (icon +

components.name: Outlined CTA Button
components.role: Primary call-to-action in header and inline contexts
components.description: Ghost button: transparent background, 1px border in #62646a, 4px radius, padding 6px 14px. Text Inter 13px weight 500, #ffffff. The border is the affordance, not a fill — this button never uses solid color, maintaining the monochrome discipline.

components.name: Command Bar
components.role: Central hero element — the product's capture interface demonstrated inline
components.description: Floating card on canvas: 8px radius, background #3b3c3 with 40% black shadow stack. Internal input area with placeholder

components.name: Serif Section Label
components.role: Small uppercase tagline above section headlines
components.description: Migra 21px weight 500, letter-spacing 0.026em, color #8a8c93. Centered above display headline. The positive tracking on serif gives it a printed-typeset quality, contrasting the sans-serif body. Used as section markers like

components.name: Serif Display Headline
components.role: Primary section headline in editorial serif
components.description: Migra 58px weight 500, letter-spacing 0.048em, line-height 1.09, color #ffffff. Centered, max-width 800px. The headline reads like a book chapter title — this is Lazy's signature type choice. Below it sits a lighter-weight subhead in the same serif.

components.name: Sans Display Headline
components.role: Hero headline that pairs with the serif subhead
components.description: Inter 55px weight 300, letter-spacing -0.045em, line-height 1.10, color #ffffff. The whisper-weight (300) is deliberate — Lazy doesn't shout. Pairs with a subhead at 16-20px Inter 400 in #8a8c93.

components.name: Note Preview Card
components.role: Embedded article/note preview in interactive sections
components.description: Light-toned card (simulating a note app): background #ffffff or very light gray, 8px radius, padding 14-24px, subtle shadow. Content includes bold title

components.name: Inline Tag
components.role: Small contextual label or category marker
components.description: Inter 12px weight 500, padding 2px 8px, 4px radius, border 1px solid #535355, color #8a8c93. Outlined only — no fill. Functions as a quiet metadata marker.

components.name: Demo Link
components.role: Inline text link to interactive product demo
components.description: Inter 14px weight 500, color #ffffff, underline on hover. Preceded by descriptive text in #8a8c93. Example:

components.name: Floating Orb / Cursor Indicator
components.role: Interactive cursor or loading indicator overlay
components.description: Circular dark element (#0a0a0a) approximately 120-160px diameter, positioned absolutely over content during interaction states. Casts no shadow itself but sits above cards to suggest a cursor following a user's attention. Unique to Lazy's interaction model.

components.name: Radial Light Wash
components.description: Concentric circular gradient emanating from center of hero — brightest at core fading to canvas. Creates a

typography.role: Display headlines and section labels — the only serif in the system, giving Lazy its editorial identity
typography.substitute: Fraunces, Source Serif Pro, or Cormorant Garamond
typography.letterSpacing: 0.026em at 21px, 0.048em at 58px — positive tracking is anti-convention for serif, making labels feel architectural and headlines feel set rather than typed

typography.role: Ultra-light display weight for large UI headlines, pairing with Migra serif in hero
typography.letterSpacing: -0.045em at 55px — extreme negative tracking compensates for thin strokes

typography.role: Body text, descriptions, and subheads — comfortable reading at 16px with generous 1.5 line-height
Navigation, labels, button text, micro-copy — the workhorse UI weight
Emphasis in body copy, strong button text, active state indicators

designSystem.description: Lazy speaks in a whisper-dark, almost monastic register: deep charcoal canvas, near-achromatic borders, and headlines set in a refined serif that feels like editorial print transplanted into a productivity tool. The entire palette stays within three values of gray — no chromatic accents, no decorative gradients on text — letting structure and serif weight carry all hierarchy. Components float on the canvas with soft, low-opacity shadows rather than hard borders, evoking a darkroom where objects glow rather than reflect. Information density is intentionally sparse; one focal element per viewport, generous negative space, and serif type at 55-58px that anchors every section like a magazine pull quote.

customSections.title: Agent Prompt Guide
customSections.content:
## Quick Color Reference
- text (primary): #ffffff
- text (muted): #8a8c93
- background: #26272c
- card surface: #3b3c3e
- border: #8a8c93 (1px)
- primary action: no distinct CTA color

customSections.title: Typography Philosophy
customSections.content: Lazy runs a two-font system: Migra (serif) for editorial moments, Inter (sans) for everything else. The serif is never used for body text, navigation, or UI labels — it appears only in display headlines and section labels. This separation creates a clear register shift: when the user sees serif, they are reading a statement; when they see Inter, they are using the product. Inter operates across four weights (300/400/500/600) with weight 300 reserved exclusively for the hero display headline — the lightest weight says

customSections.title: Shadow & Elevation Philosophy
customSections.content: Lazy uses exactly two shadow patterns and nothing else. The soft card shadow (rgba(0,0,0,0.12) 0 12px 12px) is a single-layer ambient lift for content cards. The floating UI shadow (rgba(4,4,7,0.25) 0 2px 4px + rgba(4,4,7,0.4) 0 8px 24px) is a two-layer stack for elements that float above the canvas — command bars, modals, interactive overlays. The 0.4 opacity in the second layer is high for a shadow, but it works because the canvas is already dark; the shadow reads as depth, not darkness. There are no colored shadows, no glows, no inner shadows. Elevation is communicated by lift, not by border emphasis.

designSystem.no illustrations: no decorative graphics. The product IS the visual content."
designSystem.the embedded note preview card: and the interactive cursor orb. The radial light wash in the hero is a CSS gradient
designSystem.imagery\" is simulated product UI: the floating command bar in the hero
designSystem.not a photograph. When notes or articles: they appear as light-toned cards on the dark canvas — creating a paper-on-darkroom contrast that makes content feel like a physical artifact placed in a void. There are no photographs
